app.title: ShelterBridge
app.tagline: Import a Vault*.sav file to inspect it. Your original file is never modified.

inspect.noSaves: No local Fallout Shelter saves found. Import one manually.

report.manualHint: No local game detected — copy this save to your iPhone manually (see the guide).

guide.title: Transfer to your iPhone (iMazing)
guide.intro: Fallout Shelter does not share its files via Finder. The reliable method uses iMazing, which reaches the app's data through a backup.
steps: Install iMazing on this Mac (free trial) and open it.
Connect your iPhone via USB, unlock it and tap "Trust" if asked.
Fully close Fallout Shelter on the iPhone.
In iMazing, select your iPhone then run a Backup.
Open "File System", expand Fallout Shelter → Documents folder.
Locate the iPhone's VaultX.sav (the one to replace) and make a safety copy.
Drag the VaultX.sav prepared by ShelterBridge over it (from the opened folder).
Confirm: iMazing writes the data back to the iPhone via a restore.
Launch Fallout Shelter and check the slot.

system.decoded: > Vault data decoded — original file untouched.

transfer.tagline: Choose a target slot. ShelterBridge creates a safe copy — your original is never touched.
transfer.done: Prepared {{slot}} — a safe copy; your original is untouched.
